sourceMovie.title: Us
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Us (2019) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Jordan Peele's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Us clicked for you, Psycho (1960) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Us?
faq.answer: Try Psycho, Old, Psycho II—each shares DNA with Us while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Us?
faq.answer: Us (2019) is best described as Horror, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Us worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
